comment.body:
market opened above the centroid.
alma said, above the centroid, the market is right-tailish, it literally bets on right-tail, so i will go long...
at the "check points" i check if the move still has strenght. it does? ok. i take a small profit and adjust limit-sell order.

momentum reaches the max point.
ok i take profit and let it run free, and maybe speculate on a reversal if i'm familiar with it, but carefully, bcs alma said that the righ-tail bet is real + structurally we have a gap vaccum 
i took the short, small size.
market disproved me. close it. 
end up green.

thats it

user.name: Alma

comment.body:
for someone who wants a low-risk portfolio, it is more complicated than this...

hm, market turned down pretty hard, it is illiquid and up on mechanical flows. 
Fed was hawkish, hmm, admin will potentially force Fed to fear recession, bcs labour market is not bad enough for them to cut rates and unlock MMF money.
so admin will potentially create a show for the Fed.
selling started.
+ renewing geo risk, more tariffs, more supplychain problems in the future and more uncertainity.

but wait.. recession risk is real. market actually fears it. they know. they know that the inflation is way higher than it is showed, and it comes from the supply-side (stagflation).
so we need to be careful, bcs of chainreactions... if market starts to fall too bad, and yields get unchained, investors also will start to sell, and funds will get long-squeezed...

so?? sell intraday puts heavily at the centroid of the distribution... lets create a right skewed distribution, and lever up on mechanical flows to create liquidity downward by selling vega and vomma and gamma and speed... and that will create a "stunt mats" for the spot... that will hold it up and act as a brake on the downward momentum, so it doesn't accelerate too fast, and participants will have time to readjust their positions, preventing a balckswan

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
"hm, so selling has started finally. nice. i can get in at discount prices for the new run.
oops, market rebounded 1/2 of the fall... hmm, yepp, volume is low, so bulls are not there... but vol market is active. it is all mechanical.
then this rebound is a byproduct of vol selling along with short squeezes...
i see...
bcs if traders bid downside vol and gamma, that would act like an icy road for the spot. it would expand the moves downward, so squeezing out hte shorts make sense...

hm... i can go for intraday trades, but i don't buy here...
i buy here only if the initial risk folds and the momentum proves strenght at key levels higher... but now too much uncertainity...
I buy for 5-10 years, i don't care about 3-5% moves...
this is just too risky here...
market either proves that it wants up in the next months, but then i will now it in time, and will able to buy... or it will fall downward, then my downside hegdes print on that trade, and i can buy for discount...
ok, so I'm patient"

comment.body:
Alma, i get why you ask that, but I think calling it a bull trap might be more about being afraid of a reversal than what the price is actually showing. If we call every move a trap too soon, we just might be guessing instead of watching how things play out. 

in terms of you asking what real bullish momentum is well, it shows up when buyers keep stepping in, holding higher lows, and pushing through key levels. until that breaks down, i would probably treat it as legit. Calling it a trap without real proof might just be trying to outsmart the market instead of respecting it
comment.raw_body:

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
the only thing that makes a swing trader thinking is...
what does today's move mean?

I mean, market had a bet on a certain range. (ok, in terms of volatility pricing, it was way within the range)
but the max lvls are not my imaginations. those are derived from the positioning. 
there are times when it can be broken... very rarely as you have experienced... but it is possible.

now... right tail was ok, it was expected above the centroid.
bull trap was expected 1) i posted on friday, 2) common sense: it always rebounds after such a move... 20-30 pts at least

but it broke the line that the participants were betting on... even on the weekly basis.
one thing is sure, it signals strongly forced move.
a lot of sudden shorts got squeezed out...

what would a swinger think?
well, easy: "lets let the longs running, but get some cheap otm put spreads just in case, into aug opex. but i need more information"

comment.body:
Beautiful writing Alma and you explained the styles very well. Hopefully it also stops folks from questioning others on the forum when certain traders are taken. All style of traders can make money. Your levels are clear. Above centroid and above upside pivot means it will go up that day. 

For example : Put buyers on Thursday and Friday made money. I took calls today morning and made money. It does not invalidate long term thesis and it should not matter to someone else who might have taken long term puts etc.
